description: End-to-end bug bash cycle 6. Live-validated against a real Octave 8.4 install. Found several concrete bugs: Help panel 'Error: No help found' for valid `doc sin` / `help sin` queries; WorkspacePanel needed a watchdog hack because the first refresh after a renderer reload hangs (root cause: OctaveProcessManager sometimes swallows the first command post-HMR); `doc sin` routes to Help panel but returns empty. Also: lint cleanup of leftover test hooks, publish to HTML verification, a reliable first-refresh path that does not need the watchdog.

userStories.title: Help panel shows real content for `doc sin` / `help sin`
userStories.description: Routing `doc sin` from the Command Window opens the Help panel but renders 'Error: No help found', even though the underlying `help sin` Octave command returns a full paragraph of help text. Root cause: buildHelpFetchCommand used disp(help('<name>')) — the function form of `help` returns an empty string when Octave's texinfo filter fails (e.g. Text::Unidecode missing). Fixed by using evalc('help <name>') (the command form) with a get_help_text fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing `doc sin` in the command window opens the Help panel and shows Octave's `help sin` output (starting with "'sin' is a built-in function...")
Typing `help sin` has identical behavior (both route to Help panel in MatSlop's US-031 design)
If Octave returns 'error: help: ...' the panel still renders that message verbatim instead of a generic 'No help found'
Clicking See Also cross-references navigates to the corresponding help topic
Unit test: feed the real `help sin` output (from a fixture) through the panel's content renderer and assert the displayed text
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Fixed in buildHelpFetchCommand: use evalc('help <name>') with get_help_text fallback. Added fixture tests for real Octave 8.4 help output and error-body rendering.

userStories.title: Fix WorkspacePanel first-refresh hang at source
userStories.description: The hotfix watchdog in WorkspacePanel works but masks a real bug: the very first `octaveExecute('whos')` after a renderer reload silently hangs. The OctaveProcessManager's in-flight queue likely gets into a state where the first command is lost or its response is not routed back to the awaiting IPC caller. Find the root cause in src/main/octaveProcess.ts and fix it so the watchdog becomes unnecessary.
acceptanceCriteria: After a fresh renderer reload (or first launch), `window.matslop.octaveExecute('whos')` resolves within 1 second
Watchdog in WorkspacePanel refreshWorkspace is removed (or reduced to a 30s safety net)
Unit test with a mocked process manager that simulates the race condition no longer hangs
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed in OctaveProcessManager.start(): the init-handshake pendingResolve now schedules processQueue() via setImmediate, so commands queued before the init delimiter arrives are drained. WorkspacePanel watchdog reduced from 5s to 30s as a safety net only.

userStories.title: Remove temporary `__matslopWs*` debug hooks from production build
userStories.description: The cycle-5 hotfix stack added `window.__matslopRefreshWorkspace`, `__matslopResetWsGuard`, `__matslopWsRefs` as test hooks. Gate them behind `import.meta.env.DEV` (or an E2E env flag) so they do not leak into the packaged production bundle.
acceptanceCriteria: The hooks are only assigned when `import.meta.env.DEV === true` or a test env flag is set
Existing E2E tests still find them (the test harness sets the env flag)
Production `npm run build` output contains no references to the hook names
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Wrapped the test-hook useEffect body in `if (!import.meta.env.DEV) return`. Vite's define-plugin substitutes DEV=false in production, dead-code elimination strips the entire block (strings included). Verified: `grep __matslopRefreshWorkspace dist/` returns empty after `npm run build`.

userStories.title: Verify Publish to HTML actually produces a working file end-to-end
userStories.description: US-030 added File > Publish > HTML..., but it was only verified at the unit-test level. Run an integration test where MatSlop publishes a real .mls / .m file to HTML and assert the resulting file has code blocks, text output, and at least one inline image if the script produces a figure.
acceptanceCriteria: New Playwright/E2E test creates a small .m file, opens it, triggers Publish > HTML..., and opens the resulting file
Generated HTML contains the code syntax-highlighted, the disp/fprintf output, and is self-contained (no external CSS/JS references)
If the script calls plot(), the HTML contains an inline base64 image
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Enhanced handlePublishHtml in EditorPanel to capture disp/fprintf output for .m scripts via `disp(evalc("source('<path>')"))` before building the HTML, so published docs carry real runtime output. Added tests/integration/publish-html-integration.test.ts (runs under vitest) exercising the full pipeline with real Octave + real file IO (script path + figure embedding), and a new tests/e2e/publish-html.spec.ts Playwright spec mirroring the flow through the packaged Electron app with publishSaveDialog stubbed.

userStories.title: Clean up stale debug console.log calls left behind by ralph
userStories.description: Sweep the src/ tree for leftover console.log / debugger / TODO / FIXME calls added during the polish cycles and remove any that were meant to be temporary.
acceptanceCriteria: No `console.log` calls in src/renderer/ outside of the existing error paths
No `debugger` statements
ESLint still passes on the full src tree
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Audit found no console.log/debug/info nor debugger; statements in src/. Fixed 6 lint errors that would have masked any future debug leakage: replaced 5x `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` directives (referencing an uninstalled rule) with plain `// NOTE:` comments that preserve intent, and changed `let k` → `const k` in publishHtml.ts. ESLint now reports 0 errors / 16 pre-existing warnings. 586 tests pass, typecheck clean.
